description: Workflow automation configuration for Claude Code and GitHub Copilot (multilingual)

release-prep.description: Pre-release quality gate with SRE review and status verification
release-prep.name: Release Preparation

preActions.injectContext:
Role: QC Specialist
RELEASE PREPARATION PROTOCOL:
1. SRE review for production readiness (service-layer/API changes)
2. Run pre-release quality gate  --  check:
   - Open PRs awaiting merge
   - Failing tests or builds
   - Code review completeness
   - CHANGELOG.md up-to-date
   - No known critical/major bugs
3. Generate status report with pass/fail per criterion
4. Output: PASS (clear to release) or FAIL with blocking items listed

release-prep.whenNotToUse: Rollbacks, hotfixes, release notes writing, release branch operations
release-prep.whenToUse: User wants to verify release readiness, run pre-release quality gate, or check if ready to deploy/ship

bugfix.description: Systematic debugging and fix workflow with investigation-first approach
bugfix.name: Bug Fix

preActions.injectContext:
BUG FIX PROTOCOL:
1. Scout: Find files related to the reported issue
2. Investigate: Understand current vs expected behavior
   IMPORTANT: When analyzing 'unused' code during investigation:
   - Follow Investigation Protocol (CLAUDE.md)
   - Require grep evidence, confidence ≥80%, cross-service checks (all services)
   - Use /investigate skill for removal/refactoring decisions
3. Debug: Identify root cause with evidence (file:line)
4. Plan fix with minimal blast radius
5. Validate plan before implementing
6. Validate fix rationale with /why-review
7. Fix the identified issue
8. PROVE FIX: Build code proof traces per change, confidence scores, stack-trace-style evidence. MANDATORY — never skip.
9. Update test specs — /tdd-spec UPDATE mode generates regression TCs for the bug fixed. Review with /tdd-spec-review. Sync dashboard with /test-specs-docs.
10. Simplify code for readability and consistency
11. Code review for quality and regression risk
12. Update changelog
13. Run tests to verify fix and no regressions
14. Summary report of fix and verification results

bugfix.whenNotToUse: New feature implementation, code improvement/refactoring, investigation-only (no fix), documentation updates
bugfix.whenToUse: User reports a bug, error, crash, failure, regression, or something not working; wants to fix/debug/troubleshoot an issue

feature-docs.description: Business feature documentation with 26-section template enforcement, plan validation, and mandatory test coverage
feature-docs.name: Business Feature Documentation

preActions.injectContext:
Role: Documentation Specialist
BUSINESS FEATURE DOC PROTOCOL:
- 26 sections mandatory (see template)
- Quick Navigation with Audience column
- TC-XX-XXX test case format with GIVEN/WHEN/THEN
- Evidence field with file:line format
- Cross-reference parent features if sub-feature

MANDATORY UPDATE CHECKLIST (when updating existing docs):
- ALWAYS update test sections 17-20 (Test Specifications, Test Data, Edge Cases, Regression Impact) when documenting new functionality
- ALWAYS update CHANGELOG.md with feature entry
- ALWAYS update Version History (Section 26) with new version entry
- Plan MUST include all impacted sections identified from diff analysis
- Plan MUST be validated via /plan-review and /plan-validate before any edits begin

OUTPUT: Complete 26-section README.{Feature}.md + AI companion README.{Feature}.ai.md (300-500 lines)

feature-docs.whenNotToUse: Bug fixes, feature implementation, test writing, debugging, refactoring
feature-docs.whenToUse: User wants to create or update business feature documentation in docs/business-features/

documentation.description: Documentation creation and update workflow with plan validation
documentation.name: Documentation Update

preActions.injectContext:
IMPORTANT: For business feature docs in docs/business-features/, use feature-docs workflow instead.

DOCUMENTATION UPDATE PROTOCOL:
1. Scout: Identify all documentation files affected by recent changes
2. Investigate: Read existing docs, understand current structure and content
3. Plan: List all files and sections to update with specific changes
4. Validate plan via /plan-review before making any edits
5. Execute updates following existing doc conventions and templates
6. Review changes before finalizing
7. Summary report of all documentation changes

RULES:
- For business feature docs (26-section format), use feature-docs workflow instead
- Match existing style and formatting of target documents
- Update table of contents and cross-references if structure changes
- Never create new doc files when existing ones should be updated

documentation.whenNotToUse: Feature implementation, bug fixes, test writing
documentation.whenToUse: User wants to create, update, or improve documentation, READMEs, or code comments

feature.description: Full feature development workflow with search-first approach, planning, implementation, testing, and documentation
feature.name: Feature Implementation

preActions.injectContext:
FEATURE IMPLEMENTATION PROTOCOL:
⚠️ MANDATORY: Search existing code BEFORE planning
1. Scout: Find similar features, patterns, and implementation examples using Grep/Glob
2. Investigate: Study existing patterns - validate with 3+ codebase examples (NOT generic framework docs)
3. Plan: Design solution following discovered project patterns (CQRS, state management, CSS methodology, validation, etc.)
4. Validate plan via /plan-review before any code changes
5. Validate design rationale with /why-review (features/refactors)
6. Write test specifications with /tdd-spec CREATE mode (before implementation). Review with /tdd-spec-review.
7. Update plan with test strategy via /plan (re-plan cycle). Review with /plan-review.
8. Implement with /cook (backend + frontend) — guided by test specs
9. Update test specs to catch implementation gaps with /tdd-spec UPDATE mode. Review with /tdd-spec-review. Sync dashboard with /test-specs-docs.
10. Simplify code for readability and consistency
11. Code review for quality, security, patterns compliance
12. SRE review for production readiness (service-layer/API changes)
13. Update changelog with feature entry
14. Run tests to verify no regressions
15. Update documentation if feature impacts business docs
16. Summary report of all changes

PLAN PHASES:
- PLAN₁ (after investigate): Feature design plan. Scope: architecture, file changes, implementation approach.
- PLAN₂ (after tdd-spec-review): Updated plan incorporating test strategy. Scope: refine PLAN₁ with test infrastructure, test data setup, spec coverage gaps.

GUARDRAIL: Provide file:line evidence of pattern search in plan. Follow project conventions over generic docs.

feature.whenNotToUse: Bug fixes, documentation, test-only tasks, feature requests/ideas (no implementation), PBI/story creation, design specs, large/ambiguous features needing research (use big-feature)
feature.whenToUse: User wants to implement a well-defined feature, add a component, build a capability, develop a module, implement/execute an existing plan, create a new API endpoint, or design an API contract

feature-with-integration-test.description: Feature implementation with spec-first integration testing — extends the feature workflow by adding test spec writing before implementation, plan refinement, and test verification
feature-with-integration-test.name: Feature with Integration Test

preActions.injectContext:
FEATURE WITH INTEGRATION TEST PROTOCOL:
⚠️ MANDATORY: Search existing code BEFORE planning
1. Scout: Find similar features, patterns, and implementation examples using Grep/Glob
2. Investigate: Study existing patterns - validate with 3+ codebase examples
3. Plan: Design solution following discovered project patterns
4. Validate plan via /plan-review before any code changes
5. Validate design rationale with /why-review
6. Write test specifications using /tdd-spec (feature doc Section 17) — BEFORE implementation
7. Review test specs with /tdd-spec-review for coverage and correctness
8. Update plan with test strategy via /plan (second planning round — refines original plan with test spec findings)
9. Review updated plan via /plan-review
10. Implement with /cook (backend + frontend) — guided by test specs
11. Generate integration tests from specs with /integration-test
12. Run tests to verify all TCs pass
13. Simplify code for readability and consistency
14. Code review for quality, security, patterns compliance
15. SRE review for production readiness
16. Security and performance checks
17. Update changelog with feature entry
18. Final test run to verify no regressions
19. Update documentation if feature impacts business docs
20. Summary report of all changes

PLAN PHASES:
- PLAN₁ (after investigate): Feature design plan. Scope: architecture, file changes, implementation approach.
- PLAN₂ (after tdd-spec-review): Updated plan incorporating test strategy. Scope: refine PLAN₁ with test infrastructure, test data setup, spec coverage gaps.

GUARDRAIL: This workflow enforces spec-first integration testing. Write specs → review → update plan → implement → generate tests → verify.

feature-with-integration-test.whenNotToUse: Bug fixes, documentation, test-only tasks, features without integration test needs (use feature), large/ambiguous features (use big-feature)
feature-with-integration-test.whenToUse: User wants to implement a well-defined feature AND write integration tests for it, feature with test coverage requirements, implement feature with spec-first integration testing

big-feature.description: Research-driven feature development for large, complex, or ambiguous features in an existing project — includes idea refinement, market research, business evaluation, domain analysis, tech stack research, and full implementation
big-feature.name: Big Feature (Research + Implement)

preActions.injectContext:
BIG FEATURE PROTOCOL (Research-Driven):
For large/ambiguous features in an existing codebase that need research before implementation.

MANDATORY IMPORTANT MUST RULES:
1. EVERY research stage requires AskUserQuestion validation before proceeding
2. Save artifacts to plan directory at EVERY step
3. Present 2-4 options for every major decision with confidence %
4. New Tech/Lib Gate: evaluate top 3 alternatives before adding any new dependency

STEP SELECTION GATE:
After user confirms workflow activation, present the full step list and let user deselect irrelevant ones:
- [x] Discovery Interview (idea)
- [x] Market Research (web-research)
- [x] Deep Research (deep-research)
- [x] Business Evaluation (business-evaluation)
- [x] Refine to PBI (refine)
- [x] Domain Analysis & ERD (domain-analysis)
- [x] Tech Stack Research (tech-stack-research)
- [x] User Stories (story)
- [x] Test Specifications (tdd-spec)
- [x] Test Spec Review (tdd-spec-review)
- [x] Implementation Plan (plan)
- [x] Plan Review (plan-review)
- [x] Plan Validation (plan-validate)
- [x] Design Rationale Review (why-review)
- [x] Implementation (cook)
- [x] Integration Tests (integration-test)
- [x] Code Simplification (code-simplifier)
- [x] Review Changes (review-changes)
- [x] Code Review (code-review)
- [x] SRE Review (sre-review)
- [x] Changelog (changelog)
- [x] Tests (test)
- [x] Documentation (docs-update)
- [x] Summary (watzup)

User can deselect steps (e.g., skip market research for internal features, skip business-evaluation for tech-only features).
Skipped steps should be marked as completed immediately.

PLAN PHASES (quick reference):
- PLAN₁ (after architecture-design): High-level architecture plan. Scope: system design, component boundaries, data flow, tech choices. Based on: research findings + domain analysis.
- PLAN₂ (after tdd-spec-review): Sprint-ready implementation plan. Scope: concrete tasks, file changes, test infrastructure, phased steps. Based on: stories + test specs + dependency tables.
The two plans serve different purposes — PLAN₁ is strategic, PLAN₂ is tactical.

SECOND PLANNING ROUND:
After stories + reviews are complete, a second /plan + /plan-review cycle runs.
The first /plan (after architecture-design) is high-level architecture based on research + domain analysis.
The second /plan (after tdd-spec-review) incorporates the concrete stories, test specifications, dependency tables, and refinement details into a sprint-ready implementation plan with phased steps.
This ensures the implementation plan reflects all discovered requirements, test strategy, and story dependencies.

TEST SPECIFICATIONS (after story-review, BEFORE second plan):
After stories are reviewed, write TDD specs (/tdd-spec) based on story acceptance criteria.
Review specs (/tdd-spec-review) for coverage and correctness.
The second /plan then incorporates test strategy alongside implementation tasks.

ARCHITECTURE SCAFFOLDING (after second plan-review, CONDITIONAL):
The /scaffold step is CONDITIONAL — AI must first self-investigate for existing base abstractions.
Grep for: abstract/base classes, generic interfaces, infrastructure abstractions (IRepository, IUnitOfWork), utility layers (Extensions, Helpers, Utils), frontend foundations (base component/service/store), DI registrations.
If existing scaffolding found → SKIP /scaffold step, mark completed.
If NO foundational abstractions found → PROCEED: create all base abstract classes, generic interfaces, infrastructure abstractions, and shared utilities with OOP/SOLID principles BEFORE any feature story implementation.
All infrastructure behind interfaces with at least one concrete implementation (Dependency Inversion).
For existing projects adding a new module, adapt scaffolding to extend existing base classes rather than creating duplicates.

big-feature.whenNotToUse: Small/well-defined features (use feature), new project from scratch (use greenfield-init), bug fixes, documentation, test-only tasks
big-feature.whenToUse: User wants to implement a large, complex, or ambiguous feature that needs research, market analysis, business evaluation, domain modeling, or tech stack analysis before implementation. Big new module, major enhancement, cross-cutting capability, or feature where scope is unclear

investigation.name: Code Investigation

preActions.injectContext:
INVESTIGATION PROTOCOL:
1. Scout: Find relevant files, entry points, and related code
2. Investigate: Trace code paths, understand architecture and data flow
- Output findings as structured analysis with file:line references
- Include architecture diagrams where helpful
- Identify patterns, dependencies, and potential concerns

GUARDRAIL: This is a READ-ONLY workflow. DO NOT modify any files. Only read, analyze, and report.

investigation.whenNotToUse: Any action that modifies code (implement, fix, create, refactor, test, review, document, design, plan)
investigation.whenToUse: User wants to understand how code works, find where logic lives, explore architecture, trace code paths, or get explanations

refactor.description: Code improvement and restructuring workflow with search-first approach
refactor.name: Code Refactoring

preActions.injectContext:
Role: Refactoring Specialist
REFACTORING PROTOCOL:
⚠️ MANDATORY: Search existing code BEFORE planning
1. Scout: Find similar refactoring patterns, identify target architecture examples using Grep/Glob
2. Investigate: Study existing patterns - validate with 3+ codebase examples (NOT generic framework docs)
3. Plan: Identify code smells, define target architecture following discovered project patterns
4. Validate plan  --  ensure no behavioral changes, only structural
5. Validate design rationale with /why-review (features/refactors)
6. Implement incrementally  --  small, verifiable steps
7. Verify test specs still match after refactoring with /tdd-spec update mode. Review with /tdd-spec-review. Sync dashboard with /test-specs-docs.
8. Simplify: Remove dead code, flatten nesting, extract duplicates
   CRITICAL: Before removing any code:
   - Use /investigate skill for 'unused' code verification
   - Require evidence: grep results + confidence ≥80% + cross-service validation
   - See Investigation Protocol (CLAUDE.md)
9. Code review: Verify no functional regressions
10. SRE review for production readiness (service-layer/API changes)
11. Update changelog with refactoring summary
12. Run tests  --  all existing tests MUST pass
13. Summary report of structural improvements

GUARDRAILS:
- Refactoring MUST NOT change observable behavior
- Follow project patterns: CQRS, state management, CSS methodology, validation, etc.
- Apply Code Responsibility Hierarchy: Entity > Service > Component
- Provide file:line evidence of pattern search in plan

refactor.whenToUse: User wants to restructure, reorganize, clean up, or improve existing code without changing behavior; technical debt

review-changes.description: Review uncommitted changes before commit with summary report
review-changes.name: Review Current Changes

preActions.injectContext:
PRE-COMMIT REVIEW:
- Review all staged and unstaged changes
- Check for: security issues, debug artifacts (console.log, debugger), incomplete code, style violations
- Verify no sensitive files (.env, credentials) are staged
- Check architecture compliance, naming, patterns
- Report findings with file:line references
- Output: PASS (safe to commit) or ISSUES FOUND (with list)

review-changes.whenNotToUse: PR reviews, codebase reviews, branch comparisons
review-changes.whenToUse: User wants to review current uncommitted, staged, or unstaged changes before committing

review.description: Code review and quality check workflow
review.name: Code Review

preActions.injectContext:
CODE REVIEW PROTOCOL:
1. Review code for quality, patterns compliance, security, and performance
2. Apply project standards: CQRS, state management, CSS methodology, Code Responsibility Hierarchy
3. Check docs/project-reference/code-review-rules.md for project-specific review rules
4. Report findings with severity (Critical/Major/Minor) and file:line references
5. Summarize with actionable recommendations

review.whenNotToUse: Reviewing uncommitted changes (use review-changes), reviewing plans/designs/specs/docs
review.whenToUse: User wants a code review, PR review, codebase quality audit, or code quality check

testing.name: Testing

preActions.injectContext:
TEST EXECUTION:
- Run relevant test suites for the current context
- Report results with pass/fail counts and failure details
- For failing tests, include file:line and error message
- Suggest fixes for failures when root cause is clear

testing.whenNotToUse: Test specification creation (use pbi-to-tests), test case generation from PBI
testing.whenToUse: User wants to write tests, run test suites, check test coverage, or execute tests

e2e-from-recording.description: Generate Playwright E2E tests from Chrome DevTools recordings
e2e-from-recording.name: E2E from Recording

preActions.injectContext:
E2E FROM RECORDING PROTOCOL:
1. Validate recording file exists (JSON format)
2. Identify target app and feature from user context
3. Run convert-recording.ts to generate initial test file
4. Load test specifications from feature docs Section 17
5. Map TC-XXX-XXX test cases to recording steps
6. Enhance generated code with BEM selectors
7. Add screenshot assertions at key states
8. Generate Page Object if complex flow
9. Run test to verify it passes
10. Report generated files and any manual steps needed

e2e-from-recording.whenNotToUse: Updating existing tests, writing tests from scratch, running existing tests
e2e-from-recording.whenToUse: User has a Chrome DevTools recording JSON and wants to generate a Playwright E2E test file

e2e-update-ui.description: Update E2E screenshot baselines after UI changes
e2e-update-ui.name: E2E Update UI

preActions.injectContext:
E2E UPDATE UI PROTOCOL:
1. Identify visual changes from git diff (SCSS, HTML, TS)
2. Map changed files to affected page objects
3. Find E2E specs using those page objects
4. Run affected tests to generate new screenshots
5. Update screenshot baselines with --update-snapshots
6. Visual review: diff old vs new baselines
7. Confirm changes are intentional with user
8. Report updated files and visual changes

e2e-update-ui.whenNotToUse: Generating new tests, fixing test logic, non-visual changes
e2e-update-ui.whenToUse: User made UI changes and needs to update E2E screenshot baselines

e2e-from-changes.description: Update E2E tests based on code or spec changes
e2e-from-changes.name: E2E from Changes

preActions.injectContext:
E2E FROM CHANGES PROTOCOL:
1. Detect change type from git diff:
   - Test spec changes in feature docs -> Generate new test cases
   - Code changes -> Update existing test assertions
   - API changes -> Update test data and API mocks
2. Load affected test specifications (TC-XXX-XXX)
3. Update or generate test implementations
4. Ensure traceability: each TC has corresponding test
5. Run tests to verify changes work
6. Report updated test coverage

e2e-from-changes.whenNotToUse: New recordings (use e2e-from-recording), visual-only changes (use e2e-update-ui)
e2e-from-changes.whenToUse: User updated test specifications or source code and needs to sync E2E tests

batch-operation.description: Multi-file batch operations requiring progress tracking
batch-operation.name: Batch Operation

preActions.injectContext:
BATCH OPERATION PROTOCOL:
1. Plan: List ALL files to modify, define change pattern
2. Validate plan  --  get user approval before bulk changes
3. Why-review: Challenge whether batch change is necessary (vs per-file solutions)
4. Implement: Apply changes file-by-file with progress tracking
5. Update test specs for bulk changes with /tdd-spec update mode. Review with /tdd-spec-review. Sync dashboard with /test-specs-docs.
6. Code-simplifier: KISS/DRY/YAGNI pass on all changed files
7. Review changes for correctness and completeness
8. SRE-review: Assess blast radius of bulk changes
9. Run tests after batch to catch regressions
10. Summary report with file count and change summary

SAFETY:
- ALWAYS list all affected files in plan before modifying
- Use find-and-replace patterns, not manual edits
- Checkpoint progress every 10 files
- If any file fails, STOP and report before continuing

batch-operation.whenToUse: User wants to modify multiple files at once: bulk rename, find-and-replace across codebase, update all instances

idea-to-pbi.description: PO/BA workflow: capture idea, refine to PBI, create stories, prioritize
idea-to-pbi.name: Idea to PBI

preActions.injectContext:
Role: Product Owner
IDEA-TO-PBI WORKFLOW:
1. Capture idea with problem statement
2. Refine into PBI with acceptance criteria
3. Break into user stories (GIVEN/WHEN/THEN)
4. Dev BA PIC collaborative review via /pbi-challenge
5. DoR gate validation via /dor-gate
6. Prioritize using RICE or MoSCoW

idea-to-pbi.whenNotToUse: Bug fixes, direct implementation (use feature workflow)
idea-to-pbi.whenToUse: User has a new idea, feature request, or wants to add to backlog; wants to refine idea into PBI with stories

sprint-planning.description: Sprint planning ceremony with backlog prioritization and dependency check
sprint-planning.name: Sprint Planning

preActions.injectContext:
Role: Product Owner
SPRINT PLANNING:
1. /prioritize - Review and prioritize backlog
2. /dependency - Check blockers and dependencies
3. /team-sync - Generate planning meeting agenda

sprint-planning.whenNotToUse: Sprint reviews, sprint status reports, sprint retrospectives
sprint-planning.whenToUse: User wants to plan a sprint, run backlog grooming/refinement, or kick off a new sprint

pre-development.description: Quality gate verification before development begins
pre-development.name: Pre-Development Check

preActions.injectContext:
Role: QC Specialist
PRE-DEVELOPMENT CHECK:
1. Verify PBI/story acceptance criteria clear
2. Check design specs available
3. Verify dependencies identified
4. If PASS  ->  proceed to planning
5. Validate plan before implementation

pre-development.whenNotToUse: QA checks, release prep, production readiness
pre-development.whenToUse: User wants to verify readiness before starting development: check prerequisites, quality gate, start dev

pbi-to-tests.description: QA workflow: generate TDD test specs from PBI, write to feature docs Section 17, run quality gate
pbi-to-tests.name: PBI to Tests

preActions.injectContext:
PBI-TO-TESTS WORKFLOW:
1. Read PBI acceptance criteria
2. Generate test specifications with TC-{FEATURE}-{NNN} IDs using /tdd-spec
3. Write TCs to feature doc Section 17 (canonical registry)
4. Run pre-QA quality gate

pbi-to-tests.whenNotToUse: Running existing tests, checking test results
pbi-to-tests.whenToUse: User wants to generate test specs or test cases from a PBI, feature, story, or acceptance criteria

design-workflow.description: Designer workflow: create design specification and implement UI (product, marketing, creative) from requirements or screenshots
design-workflow.name: Design Workflow

preActions.injectContext:
Role: UX Designer
DESIGN WORKFLOW:
1. Read requirements/PBI
2. Create design spec with component inventory, states, tokens, accessibility
3. DESIGN IMPLEMENTATION GATE (pick ONE, skip the other):
   - Product UIs (dashboards, admin panels, SaaS apps, data interfaces) → /interface-design (skip frontend-design)
   - Marketing pages, landing pages, creative UIs, screenshot replication → /frontend-design (skip interface-design)
   Mark the skipped step as completed immediately.
4. Review with code-review agent

design-workflow.whenNotToUse: Implementing an existing design in code
design-workflow.whenToUse: User wants to create a UI/UX design spec, mockup, wireframe, or component specification, design a product interface (dashboard, admin panel, SaaS app), build a landing page, create a marketing page, replicate a screenshot/design, or build a creative/distinctive frontend interface

pm-reporting.description: PM workflow: generate status report and dependency analysis
pm-reporting.name: PM Reporting

preActions.injectContext:
Role: Project Manager
PM REPORTING WORKFLOW:
1. Gather sprint/project data
2. Generate status report with metrics
3. Analyze dependencies and blockers
4. Output to plans/reports/

pm-reporting.whenNotToUse: Git status, build status, PR status, commit status
pm-reporting.whenToUse: User wants a status report, sprint update, project progress report, or weekly summary

verification.description: Investigate-first verification: understand context, test/check behavior, report findings with root cause, then fix only if user approves
verification.name: Verification & Validation

preActions.injectContext:
VERIFICATION WORKFLOW PROTOCOL:
1. Scout: Find files related to what needs verification
2. Investigate: Understand current behavior, trace code paths
   NOTE: If investigation reveals 'unused' code:
   - Follow Investigation Protocol (CLAUDE.md lines 302-430)
   - Use /investigate skill for evidence-based analysis
   - Require confidence ≥80% before recommending removal
3. Test (initial): Run relevant tests, check behavior, gather evidence
4. **CRITICAL GATE**: STOP and report to user:
   - What was verified
   - Current behavior vs expected behavior
   - Root cause analysis (if issue found)
   - Verdict: PASS or FAIL with evidence
5. ASK USER: 'Should I proceed to fix this?' (only if FAIL)
6. If PASS or user declines  ->  mark remaining steps completed
7. If user approves fix  ->  Plan fix with minimal blast radius
8. Implement fix  ->  Prove fix
9. Update test specs — /tdd-spec UPDATE mode generates regression TCs. Review with /tdd-spec-review. Sync dashboard with /test-specs-docs.
10. Simplify code  ->  Review changes  ->  Code review for quality
11. Run tests to verify fix and no regressions
12. Summary report of verification results and any fixes applied

verification.whenNotToUse: Bug reports (known broken), investigation-only, feature implementation, code reviews
verification.whenToUse: User wants to verify, validate, confirm, or ensure something is correct/working; sanity check or double-check

quality-audit.description: Quality audit: review artifacts for best practices, identify flaws and enhancements, fix if needed
quality-audit.name: Quality Audit

preActions.injectContext:
QUALITY AUDIT WORKFLOW:
1. Code Review: Audit artifacts for quality, best practices, completeness
2. Plan: Document findings, propose fixes and enhancements
3. Plan Review: Validate fix plan before implementation
4. Code: Implement approved fixes
5. Update test specs if fixes changed behavior with /tdd-spec update mode. Review with /tdd-spec-review.
6. Review changes for correctness
7. Test: Verify fixes don't break anything
8. Watzup: Summarize all changes made

CRITICAL GATE after code-review:
- Report findings to user with severity (Critical/Major/Minor/Suggestion)
- ASK: 'Found N issues. Should I proceed to fix?' 
- If user approves  ->  continue with plan  ->  plan-review  ->  code  ->  test  ->  watzup
- If user declines  ->  mark remaining steps completed

quality-audit.whenNotToUse: Bug fixes, feature implementation, investigation-only, reviewing uncommitted changes, PR reviews
quality-audit.whenToUse: User wants to audit code quality, review skills/commands/hooks for best practices, find flaws and suggest enhancements

deployment.name: Deployment & Infrastructure

preActions.injectContext:
Role: DevOps Engineer
DEPLOYMENT WORKFLOW:
1. Review infrastructure requirements
2. Plan deployment strategy (Docker, K8s, CI/CD)
3. Implement configuration changes
4. Review changes before deployment review
5. Verify deployment readiness

GUARDRAILS:
- Always verify rollback strategy exists
- Never modify production configs without explicit approval
- Check scripts/k8s/ for existing deployment helpers

deployment.whenNotToUse: Explaining deployment concepts, checking deployment status/history, infrastructure investigation only
deployment.whenToUse: User wants to set up or modify deployment, infrastructure, CI/CD pipelines, Docker configuration, Kubernetes setup, or deploy to environments

package-upgrade.description: Package dependency upgrade with regression verification
package-upgrade.name: Package Upgrade

preActions.injectContext:
Role: Dependency Manager
PACKAGE UPGRADE PROTOCOL:
1. Scout: Identify current versions, check for breaking changes
2. Investigate: Read changelogs, migration guides for target versions
3. Plan: List all packages to upgrade, identify breaking changes
4. Implement: Upgrade packages one at a time or in compatible groups
5. Test: Run full test suite after each upgrade group
6. Review: Check for deprecated API usage

GUARDRAILS:
- One major version bump at a time
- Read changelog for EVERY package upgraded
- Run tests after each upgrade, not just at the end
- Check peer dependency compatibility
- Keep lockfile changes in separate commit

package-upgrade.whenNotToUse: Adding new packages (use feature), removing packages (use refactor)
package-upgrade.whenToUse: User wants to upgrade packages, update dependencies, npm update, NuGet upgrade, version bump

performance.name: Performance Optimization

preActions.injectContext:
Role: Performance Engineer
PERFORMANCE PROTOCOL:
1. Scout: Identify slow endpoints/components
2. Investigate: Profile, measure baseline metrics
3. Plan: Design optimization with expected improvement targets
4. Implement: Apply optimizations
5. Update test specs for performance-impacted behavior with /tdd-spec update mode. Review with /tdd-spec-review. Sync dashboard with /test-specs-docs.
6. Test: Verify improvements with before/after metrics
7. SRE: Validate production readiness
8. Summary: Report metrics improvement

GUARDRAILS:
- Measure BEFORE and AFTER — no optimization without metrics
- Focus on p95/p99 latency, not just average
- Consider query plans for DB optimizations
- Check all services for cross-service performance impact

performance.whenNotToUse: Bug fixes (use bugfix), feature implementation, refactoring without performance goals
performance.whenToUse: User reports slow performance, latency issues, optimization needed, bottleneck investigation, query optimization

migration.description: Database schema and data migration workflow
migration.name: Database Migration

preActions.injectContext:
Role: Database Administrator
DATABASE MIGRATION PROTOCOL:
1. Analyze current schema and identify breaking changes
2. Plan rollback strategy before implementation
3. Use project data migration executor for data migrations
4. Use EF migrations for schema changes
5. Review changes before code review
6. Verify migration is idempotent (safe to re-run)

GUARDRAILS:
- Always provide rollback path
- Never delete data without backup strategy
- Test migration on dev data before production

migration.whenNotToUse: Explaining migration concepts, checking migration history/status, schema investigation only
migration.whenToUse: User wants to create or run database migrations: schema changes, data migrations, EF migrations, adding/removing/altering columns or tables

security-audit.name: Security Audit

preActions.injectContext:
Role: Security Architect
SECURITY AUDIT WORKFLOW:
1. Scan for OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities
2. Review authentication and authorization patterns
3. Check input validation and sanitization
4. Assess data protection and encryption
5. Report findings with severity ratings

GUARDRAILS:
- Read-only analysis unless fix is approved
- Use CVSS scoring for severity
- Check both frontend and backend attack surfaces

security-audit.whenNotToUse: Implementing new security features, fixing known security bugs (use bugfix workflow)
security-audit.whenToUse: User wants a security audit: vulnerability assessment, OWASP check, security review, penetration test analysis, or security compliance check

po-ba-handoff.description: PO hands off idea/PBI to BA for refinement and story creation
po-ba-handoff.name: PO to BA Handoff

preActions.injectContext:
Role: Product Owner
PO-BA HANDOFF:
1. Capture idea with /idea
2. Review artifact quality with /review-artifact
3. Create handoff record with /handoff
4. BA refines into PBI with /refine
5. BA creates user stories with /story
6. Dev BA PIC collaborative review via /pbi-challenge
7. DoR gate validation via /dor-gate

po-ba-handoff.whenNotToUse: Direct development, design-first workflows, already refined requirements
po-ba-handoff.whenToUse: PO hands off idea/PBI to BA for refinement and story creation

ba-dev-handoff.description: BA hands off refined stories to development team with quality gate
ba-dev-handoff.name: BA to Dev Handoff

preActions.injectContext:
Role: Business Analyst
BA-DEV HANDOFF:
1. Review artifact completeness with /review-artifact
2. Run quality gate with /quality-gate
3. Create handoff record with /handoff
4. Proceed to planning with /plan
5. Validate plan before implementation

ba-dev-handoff.whenNotToUse: Unrefined ideas, missing acceptance criteria, design-first features
ba-dev-handoff.whenToUse: BA hands off refined stories to development team, pre-dev quality gate

dev-qa-handoff.description: Development complete, handoff to QA for testing
dev-qa-handoff.name: Dev to QA Handoff

preActions.injectContext:
DEV-QA HANDOFF:
1. Create handoff record with /handoff
2. Generate test spec and test cases with /test-spec

dev-qa-handoff.whenNotToUse: Incomplete features, missing unit tests, untested code
dev-qa-handoff.whenToUse: Development complete, handoff to QA for testing

qa-po-acceptance.description: Testing complete, QA hands off to PO for acceptance and sign-off
qa-po-acceptance.name: QA to PO Acceptance

preActions.injectContext:
Role: QC Specialist
QA-PO ACCEPTANCE:
1. Run quality gate with /quality-gate
2. Create handoff record with /handoff
3. PO acceptance decision with /acceptance

qa-po-acceptance.whenNotToUse: Incomplete testing, known defects, missing test coverage
qa-po-acceptance.whenToUse: Testing complete, QA hands off to PO for acceptance and sign-off

design-dev-handoff.description: Designer hands off design spec to developer for implementation
design-dev-handoff.name: Designer to Dev Handoff

preActions.injectContext:
Role: UX Designer
DESIGN-DEV HANDOFF:
1. Create design spec with /design-spec
2. Review artifact with /review-artifact
3. Create handoff record with /handoff
4. Proceed to planning with /plan
5. Validate plan before implementation

design-dev-handoff.whenNotToUse: Incomplete designs, missing specs, design exploration
design-dev-handoff.whenToUse: Designer hands off design spec to developer for implementation

sprint-retro.description: End of sprint retrospective with feedback and action items
sprint-retro.name: Sprint Retrospective

preActions.injectContext:
SPRINT RETRO:
1. Generate status report with /status
2. Run retrospective with /retro
- Gather what went well, what didn't, action items
- Output to plans/reports/

sprint-retro.whenToUse: End of sprint, gather feedback, identify improvements, create action items

hotfix.description: Fast-path production fix with lightweight planning — for P0/P1 urgent issues
hotfix.name: Hotfix

preActions.injectContext:
HOTFIX PROTOCOL (URGENT):
1. Scout: Find affected code immediately
2. Plan: Lightweight plan — document what you'll fix and why
3. Plan-review: Quick sanity check on approach
4. Fix: Apply minimal, targeted fix
5. PROVE FIX: Build code proof traces per change, confidence scores. MANDATORY even for hotfixes.
6. Update test specs — /tdd-spec UPDATE mode generates regression TC for the fix. Review with /tdd-spec-review. Sync dashboard with /test-specs-docs.
7. Test: Verify fix and no regressions
8. Review: Quick review of changes
9. SRE: Verify production readiness
10. Summary: Report fix and verification

GUARDRAILS:
- Minimal blast radius — fix ONLY the broken thing
- No refactoring, no cleanup, no improvements
- Must be deployable immediately
- Planning is lightweight — document intent, not full architecture

hotfix.whenNotToUse: Non-urgent bugs (use bugfix), new features, refactoring
hotfix.whenToUse: Production emergency, P0/P1 urgent fix, hotfix, service down, critical bug in production

full-feature-lifecycle.description: Complete feature from idea to release with all role handoffs
full-feature-lifecycle.name: Full Feature Lifecycle

preActions.injectContext:
FULL FEATURE LIFECYCLE:
1. PO captures idea
2. BA refines and creates stories
3. Dev BA PIC collaborative review via /pbi-challenge
4. DoR gate validation via /dor-gate
5. Designer creates design spec
5a. DESIGN IMPLEMENTATION GATE (pick ONE, skip the other):
    - Product UIs (dashboards, admin panels, SaaS apps) → /interface-design (skip frontend-design)
    - Marketing pages, landing pages, creative UIs, screenshot replication → /frontend-design (skip interface-design)
    Mark the skipped step as completed immediately.
6. Dev plans and implements with plan validation
7. Code simplification, review changes, code review, SRE review
8. QA generates test spec
9. QC runs quality gate
10. PO signs off acceptance

This is an end-to-end workflow crossing all roles.

full-feature-lifecycle.whenNotToUse: Quick fixes, minor changes, single-role tasks, dev-only features without cross-role handoffs (use big-feature or feature)
full-feature-lifecycle.whenToUse: Complete feature from idea to release with formal role handoffs (PO→BA→Designer→Dev→QA→PO acceptance). Use when multiple team roles are involved and formal sign-off is required

research.description: Research a topic on the web, deep-dive into sources, synthesize findings into a structured report, and review quality
research.name: Research & Synthesis

preActions.injectContext:
RESEARCH & SYNTHESIS PROTOCOL:
1. Web Research: Broad search (max 10 WebSearch), collect sources, validate credibility, build source map
2. Deep Research: Fetch top sources (max 8 WebFetch), extract key findings, cross-validate claims, build evidence base
3. Knowledge Synthesis: Load evidence + template, write structured report with inline citations, citation audit
4. Knowledge Review: 7-point quality check (template, citations, confidence, sources, gaps, cross-validation, actionability)

GUARDRAILS:
- Every factual claim requires 2+ independent sources
- Never fabricate sources or citations
- Confidence scoring mandatory for all findings
- Working files in .claude/tmp/, final output in docs/knowledge/research/
- Use enforced template: docs/templates/research-report-template.md

research.whenNotToUse: Codebase investigation (use investigation), course building, marketing strategy, business evaluation
research.whenToUse: User wants to research a topic, synthesize knowledge, create a research report, learn about a subject, gather information from the web

course-building.description: Research a topic and build structured learning/teaching course material with Bloom taxonomy objectives
course-building.name: Course Material Builder

preActions.injectContext:
COURSE BUILDING PROTOCOL:
1. Web Research: Research the topic broadly (max 10 WebSearch)
2. Deep Research: Deep-dive into pedagogical sources and subject matter (max 8 WebFetch)
3. Course Builder: Build structured course with Bloom taxonomy objectives, modules, lessons, exercises, assessments
4. Knowledge Review: Quality review of course material

GUARDRAILS:
- Learning objectives must use Bloom taxonomy verbs (Remember→Understand→Apply→Analyze→Evaluate→Create)
- Each module needs clear prerequisites and outcomes
- Include practical exercises and assessments
- Working files in .claude/tmp/, final output in docs/knowledge/courses/
- Use enforced template: docs/templates/course-outline-template.md

course-building.whenNotToUse: Research reports, marketing strategy, business evaluation, code documentation
course-building.whenToUse: User wants to build a course, create learning material, design a curriculum, prepare teaching content, training program

marketing-strategy.description: Research market landscape and build a comprehensive marketing strategy with positioning, channels, campaigns, and KPIs
marketing-strategy.name: Marketing Strategy

preActions.injectContext:
MARKETING STRATEGY PROTOCOL:
1. Web Research: Research market landscape, competitors, trends (max 10 WebSearch)
2. Deep Research: Deep-dive into competitor strategies, market data, customer insights (max 8 WebFetch)
3. Market Analysis: Competitor analysis, TAM/SAM/SOM sizing, SWOT, customer segmentation
4. Strategy Builder: Positioning, channels, messaging, campaigns, budget, KPIs
5. Knowledge Review: Quality review of strategy document

GUARDRAILS:
- Market sizing must use verifiable data with source citations
- Competitor analysis needs evidence-based comparisons
- Campaign recommendations need budget estimates and KPI targets
- Working files in .claude/tmp/, final output in docs/knowledge/strategy/marketing/
- Use enforced template: docs/templates/marketing-strategy-template.md

marketing-strategy.whenNotToUse: Research reports, course building, business evaluation, code tasks
marketing-strategy.whenToUse: User wants to build a marketing strategy, analyze market landscape, plan campaigns, competitive analysis, go-to-market strategy

business-evaluation.description: Research market, evaluate business idea viability with BMC, financials, risk matrix, and execution plan
business-evaluation.name: Business Idea Evaluation

preActions.injectContext:
BUSINESS EVALUATION PROTOCOL:
1. Web Research: Research market, competitors, industry trends (max 10 WebSearch)
2. Deep Research: Deep-dive into market data, business models, financial benchmarks (max 8 WebFetch)
3. Market Analysis: TAM/SAM/SOM, competitor landscape, customer segments
4. Business Evaluation: BMC (9 blocks), 3-year financials, risk matrix (min 5 risks), execution plan (3 phases), verdict (1-10 + Pursue/Pivot/Pause/Pass)
5. Knowledge Review: Quality review of evaluation document

GUARDRAILS:
- Financial projections need stated assumptions and sensitivity analysis
- Risk matrix must include likelihood × impact scoring
- BMC must cover all 9 building blocks
- Verdict must be evidence-based with clear reasoning
- Working files in .claude/tmp/, final output in docs/knowledge/strategy/business/
- Use enforced template: docs/templates/business-evaluation-template.md

business-evaluation.whenNotToUse: Research reports, course building, marketing strategy only, code tasks
business-evaluation.whenToUse: User wants to evaluate a business idea, assess viability, create a business plan, analyze opportunity and risk, startup validation

idea-to-tdd.description: Full cycle: capture idea interactively, refine to PBI with testability assessment, generate TDD test specifications
idea-to-tdd.name: Idea to TDD Specs

preActions.injectContext:
IDEA-TO-TDD WORKFLOW:
1. Capture idea interactively with discovery interview (AskUserQuestion)
2. Refine into PBI with testability assessment
3. Generate TDD test specs in feature docs Section 17 using /tdd-spec

This workflow supports TDD-first development: write test specs BEFORE implementation.

idea-to-tdd.whenNotToUse: Direct implementation, bug fixes, existing PBIs that already have test specs
idea-to-tdd.whenToUse: User wants to go from idea to test-driven specifications, TDD approach, spec-first development, write tests before code

tdd-feature.description: Test-driven feature: write test specs first, then implement, then verify with integration tests
tdd-feature.name: TDD Feature Implementation

preActions.injectContext:
TDD FEATURE WORKFLOW:
1. Scout & investigate codebase
2. Write test specs FIRST (feature doc Section 17) using /tdd-spec
3. Plan implementation
4. Validate plan
5. Implement feature with /cook
6. Generate integration tests from specs
7. Run tests & verify all TCs pass
8. Code review and documentation

This workflow enforces test-first development: specs → plan → implement → verify.

tdd-feature.whenNotToUse: Bug fixes, quick changes, documentation-only tasks, implement-first approach
tdd-feature.whenToUse: TDD implementation, test-first development, spec-driven feature, write test specs before implementing

test-spec-update.description: Update test specs and feature docs after code changes, bug fixes, or PR reviews
test-spec-update.name: Test Spec Update (Post-Change)

preActions.injectContext:
TEST SPEC UPDATE WORKFLOW:
Use after code changes, bug fixes, or PR reviews to keep test specs in sync.
1. Review what changed (git diff or PR diff)
2. Update test specs in feature doc Section 17 using /tdd-spec update mode
3. Sync dashboard (docs/test-specs/) via /test-specs-docs
4. Generate/update integration tests for changed TCs
5. Run tests to verify

Key: /tdd-spec uses UPDATE mode — diffs existing TCs against current code, adds regression TCs for bugfixes.

test-spec-update.whenNotToUse: New feature implementation (use tdd-feature), no code changes yet, idea refinement
test-spec-update.whenToUse: After fixing a bug update test specs, after code changes update test specs, after PR review update test specs, sync test specs after changes, update test documentation after implementation

test-to-integration.description: Generate integration tests from existing test specifications in feature docs or test-specs/
test-to-integration.name: Test Specs to Integration Tests

preActions.injectContext:
TEST-TO-INTEGRATION WORKFLOW:
Generate integration tests from existing test specifications.
1. Scout: Find the relevant test spec documents and existing test files
2. Integration Test: Generate test files from TCs in feature doc Section 17
   - Each test gets test spec annotation linking to TC-{FEATURE}-{NNN}
   - Tests use real DI, no mocks (subcutaneous testing pattern)
3. Test: Build and run the generated tests
4. Verify: Check bidirectional traceability (every TC has a test, every test has a TC)

test-to-integration.whenNotToUse: No test specs exist yet (use tdd-spec first), test planning phase, documentation-only
test-to-integration.whenToUse: Generate integration tests from test specs, create tests from feature docs, implement test cases from specifications, test specs to code

test-verify.description: Comprehensive test verification: review quality, diagnose failures, verify traceability, fix flaky tests
test-verify.name: Test Verification & Quality

preActions.injectContext:
TEST VERIFICATION WORKFLOW:
Comprehensive test quality verification covering 4 concerns:
1. Scout: Find all integration test files and related specs
2. Integration Test (review mode): Audit test quality — flaky patterns, missing polling for async assertions, non-unique test data, best practice violations
3. Integration Test (verify mode): Check bidirectional traceability — every test has a TC in feature docs, every TC has a matching test, no orphans
4. Test: Run tests to identify actual failures
5. Integration Test (diagnose mode): For any failures — determine root cause: test bug vs code bug vs infrastructure issue
6. Review: Summarize findings and recommended fixes

KEY FLAKY PATTERNS TO DETECT:
- DB assertions without WaitUntilAsync/polling after async event handlers
- Hardcoded delays instead of condition-based polling
- Non-unique test data causing cross-test interference
- Race conditions from shared mutable state

MISMATCH RESOLUTION:
- Test passes but spec says different behavior → update spec
- Test fails but spec describes expected behavior → update test
- Test exists without spec → create spec from test
- Spec exists without test → generate test from spec

test-verify.whenNotToUse: Writing new tests (use test-to-integration), creating test specs (use tdd-spec), new feature implementation
test-verify.whenToUse: Review test quality, fix flaky tests, diagnose test failures, verify test traceability, test audit, test health check, integration test review, why tests fail, tests not matching specs

greenfield-init.description: Full waterfall project inception from idea through implementation with integration testing
greenfield-init.name: Greenfield Project Init

preActions.injectContext:
GREENFIELD PROJECT INCEPTION PROTOCOL:
You are acting as a Solution Architect for a brand-new project.

MANDATORY IMPORTANT MUST RULES:
1. EVERY stage requires AskUserQuestion validation before proceeding
2. Save artifacts to plan directory at EVERY step
3. All tech recommendations include confidence % and evidence
4. Present 2-4 options for every major decision
5. Delegate architecture decisions to solution-architect agent
6. After confirmFirst, present ALL steps and let user deselect irrelevant ones
7. NEVER ask tech stack upfront — business analysis first, tech stack research after domain analysis
8. Domain analysis produces ERD + bounded contexts BEFORE tech stack research
9. Tech stack research compares top 3 options per layer with detailed pros/cons

STEP SELECTION GATE:
After user confirms workflow activation, immediately present the full step list and ask which steps to include. Example:
- [x] Discovery Interview (idea)
- [x] Market Research (web-research)
- [x] Deep Research (deep-research)
- [x] Business Evaluation (business-evaluation)
- [x] Refine to PBI (refine)
- [x] Domain Analysis & ERD (domain-analysis) — NEW
- [x] Tech Stack Research (tech-stack-research) — NEW
- [x] Implementation Plan (plan)
- [x] Plan Validation (plan-validate)
- [x] Test Strategy (tdd-spec) — includes integration test strategy
- [x] User Stories (story)
- [x] Final Review (plan-review)

User can deselect steps that don't apply (e.g., skip market research for internal tools).
Skipped steps should be marked as completed immediately in the workflow.

PLAN PHASES (quick reference):
- PLAN₁ (after architecture-design): High-level architecture plan. Scope: system design, layer boundaries, component responsibilities, tech choices. Followed by /security + /performance review of the architecture.
- PLAN₂ (after tdd-spec-review): Sprint-ready implementation plan. Scope: concrete tasks, file changes, scaffolding needs, test infrastructure. Based on: stories + test specs from TDD-SPEC₁.
- PLAN₃ (after TDD-SPEC₂ post-implementation): Integration test architecture plan. Scope: test file structure, test data setup, CI integration. Based on: implementation code + updated test specs.
The three plans serve progressively detailed purposes — architecture → implementation → test infrastructure.

SECOND PLANNING ROUND:
After stories + TDD specs are generated and reviewed, a second /plan + /plan-review cycle runs.
This second plan incorporates the concrete stories, test specs, and dependency tables into a detailed implementation plan.
The first plan is high-level architecture; the second plan is sprint-ready with phased implementation steps.

ARCHITECTURE SCAFFOLDING (after second plan-review, CONDITIONAL):
The /scaffold step is CONDITIONAL — AI must first self-investigate for existing base abstractions.
Grep for: abstract/base classes, generic interfaces, infrastructure abstractions (IRepository, IUnitOfWork), utility layers (Extensions, Helpers, Utils), frontend foundations (base component/service/store), DI registrations.
If existing scaffolding found → SKIP /scaffold step, mark completed.
If NO foundational abstractions found → PROCEED: create all base abstract classes, generic interfaces, infrastructure abstractions, and shared utilities with OOP/SOLID principles BEFORE any feature story implementation.
All infrastructure behind interfaces with at least one concrete implementation (Dependency Inversion).
The scaffolded project should be copy-ready as a starter template for similar projects.

IMPLEMENTATION & INTEGRATION TESTING (after scaffold):
After scaffolding, the workflow continues with full implementation and integration testing:
1. /why-review validates design rationale before coding
2. /cook implements the feature (backend + frontend)
3. /tdd-spec writes test specifications (feature doc Section 17)
4. /tdd-spec-review validates spec coverage and correctness
5. Third /plan + /plan-review cycle plans integration test architecture
6. /integration-test generates integration tests from specs
7. /test runs all tests to verify TCs pass
8. /code-simplifier + /review-changes + /code-review for quality
9. /sre-review + /security + /performance for production readiness
10. /changelog + final /test + /docs-update + /watzup to close
This ensures greenfield projects ship with integration test coverage from day one.

greenfield-init.whenNotToUse: Existing codebase with code, bug fixes, feature implementation, refactoring existing code
greenfield-init.whenToUse: User wants to start a new project from scratch, init a greenfield project, plan a new application, research and plan before coding, bootstrap a new codebase, build something new

visualize.description: Create visual Excalidraw diagrams from codebase investigation or web research
visualize.name: Visual Diagram

preActions.injectContext:
VISUAL DIAGRAM PROTOCOL:
This workflow creates Excalidraw diagrams. Two paths based on source:

PATH A — Codebase Visualization (default if topic is about this project):
1. Scout: Find relevant files, architecture, and code patterns
2. Investigate: Trace code paths, understand relationships and data flow
3. Diagram: Generate .excalidraw file visualizing the findings

PATH B — Knowledge Visualization (if topic requires web research):
1. Web Research: Research the topic broadly (max 10 WebSearch)
2. Deep Research: Deep-dive into top sources (max 8 WebFetch)
3. Diagram: Generate .excalidraw file visualizing the synthesized knowledge

GUARDRAILS:
- Ask user which path (A or B) if ambiguous
- Output .excalidraw files to docs/diagrams/ (create dir if needed)
- Use kebab-case filenames describing the diagram subject
- MUST render and validate diagram (render-view-fix loop)
- Read references/color-palette.md and references/element-templates.md before generating

visualize.whenNotToUse: Text-only documentation, code implementation, bug fixes, non-visual outputs
visualize.whenToUse: User wants to visualize, diagram, draw, or create visual representation of workflows, architectures, concepts, systems, or research findings
